mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) captures contemporaneous expansion in aggregate output. Rising production volumes translate into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is negative and statistically significant at the 5% level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 2.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and corporate borrowing costs. This compression of equity multiples and expected earnings produces negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, the average S&P 500 60-day forward return is non-negative, or the t-test against zero returns a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicate demand-driven labor market overheating. The interaction predicts negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns because margin pressure and expected monetary tightening reduce equity valuations.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while UNRATE falls, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, rejecting the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal demand momentum exhaustion. During late-cycle expansions, this contraction predicts negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns because inventory corrections trigger rapid earnings estimate revisions.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines month-over-month during late-cycle months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated levels of housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong residential investment and construction activity that spills into durable goods and credit growth. Sustained strength predicts positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns through earnings expansion in cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between monthly HOUST levels and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand. This flow-through to top-line corporate revenues predicts positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Monthly increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a decline in aggregate demand and productive output. This revenue contraction predicts negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns as earnings expectations are revised downward across sectors.
failure_condition: Quarters with negative GDPC1 growth produce positive average S&P 500 60-day forward returns, or the difference from baseline is insignificant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate below 25 basis points signals maximal monetary accommodation and abundant market liquidity. This carry-friendly environment predicts positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns as risk assets reprice on low discount rates.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered observations over a 3-year rolling window.
